_comment: Machine-readable dynamism-feature taxonomy (Lane B of docs/design/foundation/24_ecosystem_compat_gap_audit.md), encoded as DATA so library compatibility is DERIVABLE per binding policy amendment A. This file is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for feature status; package verdicts in package_triage.json are COMPUTED as the min over required features looked up here. Do NOT hand-edit a package verdict. To change a verdict you change a feature status here (with evidence) and re-derive. Enforced by tools/check_ecosystem_compat.py. See tools/ecosystem/README.md.

_status_vocabulary.supported: Feature is supported natively, with file:line evidence in the tree. -> verdict class 'compatible'.
_status_vocabulary.typed-shim: Forbidden in raw form, but molt re-expresses its intent through a supported primitive; the shim is a first-class, tested artifact. -> verdict class 'compatible-via-typed-shim'.
_status_vocabulary.bridge: Reachable only through the (gated, allowlisted) CPython-ABI dlopen bridge or libmolt recompile. -> verdict class 'compatible-via-bridge'. NOTE: the bridge vs 0215-spec is an OPEN USER DECISION (doc 24 OQ1/OQ2); verdicts that reduce to this class carry a policy-decision-pending note.
_status_vocabulary.partial: Doc 24 marks the feature PARTIAL: works at import / on a pure subset, but not on the full required call path; a named arc/baton converts it to a higher class. -> verdict class 'partial'. Fail-closed: a partial-required package is NOT claimed 'compatible'.
_status_vocabulary.unsupported: Explicit verified-subset exclusion (today). MUST name 'excluded_feature' and 'tracking'. -> verdict class 'incompatible-by-design'.

_interpretation_notes: INTERPRETATION (flagged): doc 24's mission enumerates FOUR verdict classes (compatible / compatible-via-typed-shim / compatible-via-bridge / incompatible-by-design), but doc 24 Lane C assigns the verdict 'PARTIAL' to ~8 of the 25 packages (import-green / pure-path-OK, blocked on a named arc for full function). Collapsing those into any of the four would either falsely assert support (compatible*) or falsely assert a permanent exclusion (incompatible-by-design). To stay fail-closed AND faithful to doc 24's findings, a FIFTH verdict class 'partial' is introduced, ordered strictly between 'compatible-via-bridge' and 'incompatible-by-design'. The four mission classes remain the canonical-named set; 'partial' is the honest representation of doc 24's PARTIAL rows. This is the one taxonomy interpretation added to make the audit machine-checkable.

INTERPRETATION (flagged): D20 (async/await) is 'compatible (runtime-light) / PARTIAL (runtime-heavy, wasm)' in doc 24. A single feature cannot hold two statuses, and the fail-closed rule forbids rounding the dual state UP to 'supported'. D20 is encoded as 'partial' (the worse of the two states governs, since a package that requires async on the runtime-heavy/wasm path is not reliably compatible). The native runtime-light support is recorded in notes.

INTERPRETATION (flagged): D7 ('compatible (registry)') and D8/D9 ('PARTIAL -> shim-able') — D7 is encoded 'supported' (doc 24 LANE A.2 lists abc.register as PRESENT via the registry path); D8 and D9 are encoded 'partial' (doc 24 explicitly: 'full structural subtyping incomplete' / '_ProtocolMeta instancecheck partial'). 'shim-able' is the future arc, not today's status; fail-closed keeps them 'partial' today.

INTERPRETATION (flagged): D17 (import-time monkeypatching) is doc-24 'PARTIAL' -> encoded 'partial'. D16/D19/D26 are 'incompatible-by-design (TODAY) -> shim-able/arc'; the TODAY status governs (fail-closed), encoded 'unsupported' with the conversion arc recorded in 'tracking'.

D1.name: Decorators (static + parametrized + stateful)
D1.evidence: runtime call/closure path; tests/runtime_compat/scripts/test_tenacity.py proves a real retry decorator runs

D4.name: Metaclasses (__new__/__init__/__prepare__, conflict resolution)
D4.notes: Used by Django models, SQLAlchemy declarative base, abc. CORRECTS doc 17 (which called this 'likely unsupported').

D5.name: __init_subclass__
D5.notes: Used by pydantic v2, plugin registries.

D7.name: ABCs + abc.register virtual subclasses
D7.notes: Used by collections.abc consumers. Doc 24 'compatible (registry)'; the registry path is PRESENT per LANE A.2.

D8.name: __subclasshook__ structural checks
D8.evidence: runtime/molt-runtime/src/builtins/abc.rs hook path; full structural subtyping incomplete
D8.notes: Used by typing Protocols, abc. Doc 24: 'PARTIAL -> shim-able'.
D8.tracking: doc 24 LANE D rank 7 (D9/D8 Protocol runtime_checkable + __subclasshook__); convert PARTIAL -> supported via structural-subtyping shim.

D9.name: @runtime_checkable Protocol isinstance
D9.notes: Used by pydantic, beartype. Doc 24: 'PARTIAL -> shim-able'.
D9.tracking: doc 24 LANE D rank 7 (D9/D8 Protocol runtime_checkable + __subclasshook__).

D10.name: Class introspection: __annotations__, get_type_hints (no forward-ref strings)
D10.notes: Used by attrs, pydantic, cattrs, dataclasses-json. CORRECTS doc 17 (which listed get_type_hints as 'pending').

D11.name: Forward-ref string annotation eval (get_type_hints on "Foo")
D11.notes: Used by pydantic v2 (heavy), SQLAlchemy 2.0 Mapped[]. Mitigated by `from __future__ import annotations` + a typed resolver. Doc 24: 'incompatible-by-design (subset: eval) unless a typed resolver shim lands'.
D11.tracking: doc 24 LANE F Arc 4 (D11 forward-ref string annotation resolver, no general eval); LANE D rank 4.

D12.name: inspect.signature / Parameter binding
D12.notes: Used by click, FastAPI-style DI, pytest. CORRECTS doc 17 (which listed inspect.signature among pending items).

D13.name: inspect.getsource / frame/source reflection
D13.evidence: no source object in AOT binary; src/molt/stdlib/inspect.py is signature/Parameter-only (LANE A.4: getsource not viable in AOT)
D13.notes: Used by IPython, some test libs. Doc 24: 'incompatible-by-design (unrestricted reflection)'.
D13.tracking: doc 24 verified-subset policy boundary (LANE A.5) — permanent exclusion, no conversion arc; AOT binary has no source object. Tracked as a documented design exclusion, not a gap.

D14.name: Controlled string-exec codegen (dataclasses/namedtuple/attrs-__init__)
D14.notes: Used by dataclasses, NamedTuple, attrs (slotted). The canonical compatible-via-typed-shim template. functionally tested (tests/runtime_compat/scripts/test_attrs.py).

D15.name: Arbitrary user exec/eval/compile
D15.notes: Used by jinja2 compiled-template path, simpleeval, marshmallow custom. Doc 24: 'incompatible-by-design'.
D15.tracking: doc 24 LANE A.5 verified-subset policy boundary — permanent exclusion (CLAUDE.md: no exec/eval/compile). Tracked as a documented design exclusion, not a gap.

D16.name: Module-level __getattr__ (PEP 562) — lazy top-level imports
D16.evidence: searched ops.rs/platform.rs/frontend: no module-level __getattr__ dispatch found (all __getattr__ hits are class/instance) — LANE A.2 gap row
D16.notes: Used by numpy, scipy, tensorflow, rich, sqlalchemy top-level. Doc 24: 'incompatible-by-design (TODAY) -> shim-able (PRIORITY)'. Highest-ROI single feature (LANE D rank 3).
D16.tracking: doc 24 LANE F Arc 2 (D16 module-level __getattr__ PEP 562: runtime module-attr fallback hook + frontend lowering); LANE D rank 3 (low-medium effort, large unlock).

D17.name: Import-time monkeypatching (module.__dict__[x]=... at import)
D17.evidence: static graph cannot reflect runtime mutation; deterministic import-time mutation may be capturable (LANE B.1 row)
D17.notes: Used by six, future, eventlet (import-time patch). Doc 24: 'PARTIAL'.
D17.tracking: doc 24 LANE B.1 D17 — characterize which import-time mutations are deterministically capturable vs genuinely runtime; no dedicated arc yet (planning input).

D18.name: Runtime monkeypatching / mock.patch / fixture injection
D18.evidence: src/molt/compat.py + CLAUDE.md verified-subset exclusion (no runtime monkeypatching)
D18.notes: Used by pytest fixtures, unittest.mock-heavy, gevent monkey. Doc 24: 'incompatible-by-design'.
D18.tracking: doc 24 LANE A.5 verified-subset policy boundary — permanent exclusion (CLAUDE.md: no runtime monkeypatching). Tracked as a documented design exclusion, not a gap.

D19.name: Generators / yield / yield from / async generators
D19.evidence: excluded today; generator-fusion keystone docs/design/generator_fusion.md is the planned lift
D19.notes: Used by trio, tenacity (some), SQLAlchemy, starlette, tornado. Doc 24: 'incompatible-by-design (TODAY) -> keystone arc'. Largest shared blocker (LANE D rank 1).
D19.tracking: doc 24 LANE F Arc 5 (D19 generators) + docs/design/generator_fusion.md keystone; LANE D rank 1 (very high effort, 50+ pkgs).

D20.name: async/await core
D20.evidence: event loop/tasks/futures OK (runtime-light); 4/5 runtime-heavy wasm fail (doc 16) — LANE B.1 row
D20.notes: Used by httpx, anyio, asyncio apps. Doc 24: 'compatible (runtime-light) / PARTIAL (runtime-heavy, wasm)'. Encoded 'partial' (worse state governs, fail-closed); native runtime-light path is supported.
D20.tracking: doc 24 LANE D rank 6 (D20 asyncio runtime-heavy + wasm) + docs/design/foundation/18_asyncio-wasm-event-loop-fix-plan.md.

D21.name: threading primitives
D21.evidence: core present; full primitive parity pending; wasm no threads (LANE B.1 row)
D21.tracking: doc 24 LANE B.1 D21 — full threading-primitive parity arc (pending); wasm threads out of scope.

D22.name: ctypes / cffi FFI
D22.evidence: src/molt/stdlib/ctypes/ real port (0 NotImplementedError in __init__.py); FFI depth is a compile-probe item; wasm no dynamic link (LANE A.6 / B.1)
D22.notes: Used by cryptography(cffi), bcrypt, PIL-via-cffi. Doc 24: 'PARTIAL'.
D22.tracking: doc 24 LANE D rank 9 (D22 ctypes/cffi FFI completeness) + COMPILE-PROBE item 7 (CDLL/Structure/c_int round-trip).

D23.name: CPython C-API extension (binary .so)
D23.evidence: runtime/molt-cpython-abi/ Track-2 dlopen bridge (~6258 lines, gated feature cext_loader, runtime-wired platform.rs:3652); OR Track-1 libmolt recompile (docs/spec/areas/tooling/0215_MOLT_EXTENSION_BUILD_PIPELINE.md)
D23.notes: Used by numpy, scipy, pandas, lxml, pillow, orjson, pydantic-core. Doc 24: 'compatible-via-bridge (gated, unproven)'. POLICY DECISION PENDING: 0215 spec says 'no CPython ABI compatibility' but the bridge does exactly that (doc 24 OQ1/OQ2).
D23.tracking: doc 24 LANE F Arc 3 (finish + prove + reconcile-policy the CPython-ABI bridge: real-wheel proof + two-track policy doc + capability gating); LANE D rank 2. Bridge is ~70% built, gated OFF, unproven against a real published wheel.

D24.name: Entry-points / plugin discovery (importlib.metadata.entry_points)
D24.notes: Used by pytest plugins, click plugins, flake8.

D25.name: Exception __traceback__ chain object model
D25.evidence: intrinsic format_exception/extract_tb (doc 16); full traceback object pending (LANE B.1 row)
D25.notes: Used by logging, pytest, sentry-style. Doc 24: 'PARTIAL'.
D25.tracking: doc 24 LANE F Arc 5 (D25 traceback object model) + LANE D rank 8.

D26.name: Regex advanced (lookahead, named groups, backrefs, flag scoping)
D26.evidence: native engine raises NotImplementedError; host fallback disabled (doc 16) — LANE B.1 row
D26.notes: Used by pyyaml, pathspec, parsers. Doc 24: 'incompatible-by-design (TODAY) -> engine arc'.
D26.tracking: doc 24 LANE F Arc 5 (D26 regex) + LANE D rank 5 (native engine feature arc).

D27.name: sys.modules / __path__ rewriting at runtime
D27.evidence: src/molt/compat.py + CLAUDE.md verified-subset exclusion (no runtime import-graph mutation)
D27.notes: Used by namespace plugins doing runtime path tricks. Doc 24: 'incompatible-by-design'.
D27.tracking: doc 24 LANE A.5 verified-subset policy boundary — permanent exclusion. Tracked as a documented design exclusion, not a gap.
